s3DeepLink.documentation: The URL to a build log in an S3 bucket.

cache.documentation: Information about the cache for the build project.

environment.documentation: Information about the build environment for this build project.

timeoutInMinutes.documentation: How long, in minutes, from 5 to 2160 (36 hours), for CodeBuild to wait before timing out any related build that did not get marked as completed. The default is 60 minutes.

queuedTimeoutInMinutes.documentation: The number of minutes a build is allowed to be queued before it times out.

encryptionKey.documentation:
The Key Management Service customer master key (CMK) to be used for encrypting the build output artifacts.

These tags are available for use by Amazon Web Services services that support CodeBuild build project tags.

created.documentation: When the build project was created, expressed in Unix time format.

lastModified.documentation: When the build project's settings were last modified, expressed in Unix time format.

vpcConfig.documentation: Information about the VPC configuration that CodeBuild accesses.

logsConfig.documentation: Information about logs for the build project. A project can create logs in CloudWatch Logs, an S3 bucket, or both.

concurrentBuildLimit.documentation:
The maximum number of concurrent builds that are allowed for this project.

New builds are only started if the current number of builds is less than or equal to this limit. If the current build count meets this limit, new builds are throttled and are not run.

publicProjectAlias.documentation: Contains the project identifier used with the public build APIs.

resourceAccessRole.documentation: The ARN of the IAM role that enables CodeBuild to access the CloudWatch Logs and Amazon S3 artifacts for the project's builds.

autoRetryLimit.documentation: The maximum number of additional automatic retries after a failed build. For example, if the auto-retry limit is set to 2, CodeBuild will call the RetryBuild API to automatically retry your build for up to 2 additional times.

PRIVATE

The credential can use the name of the credentials only if they exist in your current Amazon Web Services Region.

credentialProvider.documentation: The service that created the credentials to access a private Docker registry. The valid value, SECRETS_MANAGER, is for Secrets Manager.

Report.documentation: Information about the results from running a series of test cases during the run of a build project. The test cases are specified in the buildspec for the build project using one or more paths to the test case files. You can specify any type of tests you want, such as unit tests, integration test

arn.documentation: The ARN of the report run.

type.documentation:
The type of the report that was run.

CODE_COVERAGE

A code coverage report.

TEST

A test report.

name.documentation: The name of the report that was run.

reportGroupArn.documentation: The ARN of the report group associated with this report.

executionId.documentation: The ARN of the build run that generated this report.

created.documentation: The date and time this report run occurred.

expired.documentation: The date and time a report expires. A report expires 30 days after it is created. An expired report is not available to view in CodeBuild.

exportConfig.documentation: Information about where the raw data used to generate this report was exported.

truncated.documentation: A boolean that specifies if this report run is truncated. The list of test cases is truncated after the maximum number of test cases is reached.

testSummary.documentation: A TestReportSummary object that contains information about this test report.

codeCoverageSummary.documentation: A CodeCoverageReportSummary object that contains a code coverage summary for this report.

ReportExportConfig.documentation: Information about the location where the run of a report is exported.

exportConfigType.documentation:
The export configuration type. Valid values are:

  • S3: The report results are exported to an S3 bucket.

  • NO_EXPORT: The report results are not exported.

s3Destination.documentation: A S3ReportExportConfig object that contains information about the S3 bucket where the run of a report is exported.

ReportFilter.documentation: A filter used to return reports with the status specified by the input status parameter.

status.documentation: The status used to filter reports. You can filter using one status only.

ReportGroup.documentation: A series of reports. Each report contains information about the results from running a series of test cases. You specify the test cases for a report group in the buildspec for a build project using one or more paths to the test case files.

type.documentation:
The type of the ReportGroup. This can be one of the following values:

CODE_COVERAGE

The report group contains code coverage reports.

TEST

The report group contains test reports.

exportConfig.documentation: Information about the destination where the raw data of this ReportGroup is exported.

created.documentation: The date and time this ReportGroup was created.

lastModified.documentation: The date and time this ReportGroup was last modified.

tags.documentation:
A list of tag key and value pairs associated with this report group.

These tags are available for use by Amazon Web Services services that support CodeBuild report group tags.

status.documentation:
The status of the report group. This property is read-only.

This can be one of the following values:

ACTIVE

The report group is active.

DELETING

The report group is in the process of being deleted.

ReportGroupTrendStats.documentation: Contains trend statistics for a set of reports. The actual values depend on the type of trend being collected. For more information, see .

average.documentation: Contains the average of all values analyzed.

ReportWithRawData.documentation: Contains the unmodified data for the report. For more information, see .

data.documentation: The value of the requested data field from the report.

ResolvedArtifact.documentation: Represents a resolved build artifact. A resolved artifact is an artifact that is built and deployed to the destination, such as Amazon S3.

ResourceAlreadyExistsException.documentation: The specified Amazon Web Services resource cannot be created, because an Amazon Web Services resource with the same settings already exists.

ResourceNotFoundException.documentation: The specified Amazon Web Services resource cannot be found.

id.documentation: Specifies the identifier of the batch build to restart.

retryType.documentation: Specifies the type of retry to perform.

id.documentation: Specifies the identifier of the build to restart.

S3LogsConfig.documentation: Information about S3 logs for a build project.

status.documentation:
The current status of the S3 build logs. Valid values are:

  • ENABLED: S3 build logs are enabled for this build project.

  • DISABLED: S3 build logs are not enabled for this build project.

location.documentation: The ARN of an S3 bucket and the path prefix for S3 logs. If your Amazon S3 bucket name is my-bucket, and your path prefix is build-log, then acceptable formats are my-bucket/build-log or arn:aws:s3:::my-bucket/build-log.

encryptionDisabled.documentation: Set to true if you do not want your S3 build log output encrypted. By default S3 build logs are encrypted.

S3ReportExportConfig.documentation: Information about the S3 bucket where the raw data of a report are exported.

bucket.documentation: The name of the S3 bucket where the raw data of a report are exported.

bucketOwner.documentation: The Amazon Web Services account identifier of the owner of the Amazon S3 bucket. This allows report data to be exported to an Amazon S3 bucket that is owned by an account other than the account running the build.

path.documentation: The path to the exported report's raw data results.

packaging.documentation:
The type of build output artifact to create. Valid values include:

  • NONE: CodeBuild creates the raw data in the output bucket. This is the default if packaging is not specified.

  • ZIP: CodeBuild creates a ZIP file with the raw data in

encryptionKey.documentation: The encryption key for the report's encrypted raw data.

encryptionDisabled.documentation: A boolean value that specifies if the results of a report are encrypted.

SSMSession.documentation: Contains information about the Session Manager session.

tokenValue.documentation: An encrypted token value containing session and caller information.

streamUrl.documentation: A URL back to SSM Agent on the managed node that the Session Manager client uses to send commands and receive output from the node.

requestTime.documentation: When the sandbox process was initially requested, expressed in Unix time format.

startTime.documentation: When the sandbox process started, expressed in Unix time format.

endTime.documentation: When the sandbox process ended, expressed in Unix time format.

sourceVersion.documentation: Any version identifier for the version of the sandbox to be built.

timeoutInMinutes.documentation: How long, in minutes, from 5 to 2160 (36 hours), for CodeBuild to wait before timing out this sandbox if it does not get marked as completed.

queuedTimeoutInMinutes.documentation: The number of minutes a sandbox is allowed to be queued before it times out.

encryptionKey.documentation: The Key Management Service customer master key (CMK) to be used for encrypting the sandbox output artifacts.

serviceRole.documentation: The name of a service role used for this sandbox.

startTime.documentation: When the sandbox session started, expressed in Unix time format.

endTime.documentation: When the sandbox session ended, expressed in Unix time format.

resolvedSourceVersion.documentation: An identifier for the version of this sandbox's source code.

phaseStatus.documentation:
The current status of the sandbox phase. Valid values include:

FAILED

The sandbox phase failed.

FAULT

The sandbox phase faulted.

IN_PROGRESS

The sandbox phase is still in progress.

STOPPED

T

endTime.documentation: When the sandbox phase ended, expressed in Unix time format.

durationInSeconds.documentation: How long, in seconds, between the starting and ending times of the sandbox's phase.

ScalingConfigurationInput.documentation: The scaling configuration input of a compute fleet.

scalingType.documentation: The scaling type for a compute fleet.

maxCapacity.documentation: The maximum number of instances in the ﬂeet when auto-scaling.

scalingType.documentation: The scaling type for a compute fleet.

maxCapacity.documentation: The maximum number of instances in the ﬂeet when auto-scaling.

desiredCapacity.documentation: The desired number of instances in the ﬂeet when auto-scaling.

ScopeConfiguration.documentation: Contains configuration information about the scope for a webhook.

name.documentation: The name of either the group, enterprise, or organization that will send webhook events to CodeBuild, depending on the type of webhook.

domain.documentation: The domain of the GitHub Enterprise organization or the GitLab Self Managed group. Note that this parameter is only required if your project's source type is GITHUB_ENTERPRISE or GITLAB_SELF_MANAGED.

scope.documentation: The type of scope for a GitHub or GitLab webhook. The scope default is GITHUB_ORGANIZATION.

SourceAuth.documentation: Information about the authorization settings for CodeBuild to access the source code to be built.

type.documentation: The authorization type to use. Valid options are OAUTH, CODECONNECTIONS, or SECRETS_MANAGER.

resource.documentation: The resource value that applies to the specified authorization type.

SourceCredentialsInfo.documentation: Information about the credentials for a GitHub, GitHub Enterprise, GitLab, GitLab Self Managed, or Bitbucket repository.

arn.documentation: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the token.

serverType.documentation: The type of source provider. The valid options are GITHUB, GITHUB_ENTERPRISE, GITLAB, GITLAB_SELF_MANAGED, or BITBUCKET.

authType.documentation: The type of authentication used by the credentials. Valid options are OAUTH, BASIC_AUTH, PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN, CODECONNECTIONS, or SECRETS_MANAGER.

resource.documentation: The connection ARN if your authType is CODECONNECTIONS or SECRETS_MANAGER.

secondarySourcesVersionOverride.documentation: An array of ProjectSourceVersion objects that override the secondary source versions in the batch build project.

sourceVersion.documentation:
The version of the batch build input to be built, for this build only. If not specified, the latest version is used. If specified, the contents depends on the source provider:

A buildspec file declaration that overrides, for this build only, the latest one already defined in the build project.

The insecure SSL setting determines whether to ignore SSL warnings while connecting to the project source code. This override applies only if the build's source is GitHub Enterprise.

certificateOverride.documentation: The name of a certificate for this batch build that overrides the one specified in the batch build project.

serviceRoleOverride.documentation: The name of a service role for this batch build that overrides the one specified in the batch build project.

privilegedModeOverride.documentation: Enable this flag to override privileged mode in the batch build project.

buildTimeoutInMinutesOverride.documentation: Overrides the build timeout specified in the batch build project.

queuedTimeoutInMinutesOverride.documentation: The number of minutes a batch build is allowed to be queued before it times out.

The Key Management Service customer master key (CMK) that overrides the one specified in the batch build project. The CMK key encrypts the build output artifacts.

registryCredentialOverride.documentation: A RegistryCredential object that overrides credentials for access to a private registry.

The type of credentials CodeBuild uses to pull images in your batch build. There are two valid values:

CODEBUILD

Specifies that CodeBuild uses its own credentials. This requires that you modify your ECR repository policy to trust CodeBuild's service principal.

buildBatchConfigOverride.documentation: A BuildBatchConfigOverride object that contains batch build configuration overrides.

projectName.documentation: The name of the CodeBuild build project to start running a build.

build.documentation: Information about the build to be run.

command.documentation: The command that needs to be executed.

id.documentation: The identifier of the batch build to stop.

Tag.documentation:
A tag, consisting of a key and a value.

This tag is available for use by Amazon Web Services services that support tags in CodeBuild.

TargetTrackingScalingConfiguration.documentation: Defines when a new instance is auto-scaled into the compute fleet.

targetValue.documentation: The value of metricType when to start scaling.

TestCase.documentation: Information about a test case created using a framework such as NUnit or Cucumber. A test case might be a unit test or a configuration test.

reportArn.documentation: The ARN of the report to which the test case belongs.

testRawDataPath.documentation: The path to the raw data file that contains the test result.

prefix.documentation: A string that is applied to a series of related test cases. CodeBuild generates the prefix. The prefix depends on the framework used to generate the tests.

name.documentation: The name of the test case.

status.documentation: The status returned by the test case after it was run. Valid statuses are SUCCEEDED, FAILED, ERROR, SKIPPED, and UNKNOWN.

durationInNanoSeconds.documentation: The number of nanoseconds it took to run this test case.

message.documentation: A message associated with a test case. For example, an error message or stack trace.

expired.documentation: The date and time a test case expires. A test case expires 30 days after it is created. An expired test case is not available to view in CodeBuild.

testSuiteName.documentation: The name of the test suite that the test case is a part of.

TestCaseFilter.documentation: A filter used to return specific types of test cases. In order to pass the filter, the report must meet all of the filter properties.

status.documentation:
The status used to filter test cases. A TestCaseFilter can have one status. Valid values are:

  • SUCCEEDED

  • FAILED

  • ERROR

  • SKIPPED

  • UNK

keyword.documentation: A keyword that is used to filter on the name or the prefix of the test cases. Only test cases where the keyword is a substring of the name or the prefix will be returned.

total.documentation: The number of test cases in this TestReportSummary. The total includes truncated test cases.

statusCounts.documentation: A map that contains the number of each type of status returned by the test results in this TestReportSummary.

durationInNanoSeconds.documentation: The number of nanoseconds it took to run all of the test cases in this report.

baseCapacity.documentation: The initial number of machines allocated to the compute ﬂeet, which deﬁnes the number of builds that can run in parallel.

environmentType.documentation:
The environment type of the compute fleet.

Information about the compute resources the compute fleet uses. Available values include:

  • ATTRIBUTE_BASED_COMPUTE: Specify the amount of vCPUs, memory, disk space, and the type of machine.

computeConfiguration.documentation: The compute configuration of the compute fleet. This is only required if computeType is set to ATTRIBUTE_BASED_COMPUTE or CUSTOM_INSTANCE_TYPE.

scalingConfiguration.documentation: The scaling configuration of the compute fleet.

overflowBehavior.documentation:
The compute fleet overflow behavior.

  • For overflow behavior QUEUE, your overflow builds need to wait on the existing fleet instance to become available.

  • For overflow behavior ON_DEMAND, your overflow builds run on CodeBuild on-demand.

proxyConfiguration.documentation: The proxy configuration of the compute fleet.

imageId.documentation: The Amazon Machine Image (AMI) of the compute fleet.

fleetServiceRole.documentation: The service role associated with the compute fleet. For more information, see Allow a user t

A list of tag key and value pairs associated with this compute fleet.

The name of the build project.

You cannot change a build project's name.

description.documentation: A new or replacement description of the build project.

source.documentation: Information to be changed about the build input source code for the build project.

A version of the build input to be built for this project. If not specified, the latest version is used. If specified, it must be one of:

cache.documentation: Stores recently used information so that it can be quickly accessed at a later time.

environment.documentation: Information to be changed about the build environment for the build project.

serviceRole.documentation: The replacement ARN of the IAM role that enables CodeBuild to interact with dependent Amazon Web Services services on behalf of the Amazon Web Services account.

queuedTimeoutInMinutes.documentation: The number of minutes a build is allowed to be queued before it times out.

These tags are available for use by Amazon Web Services services that support CodeBuild build project tags.

vpcConfig.documentation: VpcConfig enables CodeBuild to access resources in an Amazon VPC.

badgeEnabled.documentation: Set this to true to generate a publicly accessible URL for your project's build badge.

logsConfig.documentation: Information about logs for the build project. A project can create logs in CloudWatch Logs, logs in an S3 bucket, or both.

The maximum number of concurrent builds that are allowed for this project.

autoRetryLimit.documentation: The maximum number of additional automatic retries after a failed build. For example, if the auto-retry limit is set to 2, CodeBuild will call the RetryBuild API to automatically retry your build for up to 2 additional times.

project.documentation: Information about the build project that was changed.

projectArn.documentation: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the build project.

  • S3: The report results are exported to an S3 bucket.

  • NO_EXPORT: The report results are not exported.

An updated list of tag key and value pairs associated with this report group.

These tags are available for use by Amazon Web Services services that support CodeBuild report group tags.

reportGroup.documentation: Information about the updated report group.

The scope configuration for global or organization webhooks.

Global or organization webhooks are only available for GitHub and Github Enterprise webhooks.

The status of the webhook. Valid values include:

  • CREATING: The webhook is being created.

  • CREATE_FAILED: The webhook has failed to create.

  • ACTIVE: The webhook has succeeded and is active.

statusMessage.documentation: A message associated with the status of a webhook.
